title: OMG Historical inaccuracy
wikitext:
[]: OMG!! Historical inaccuracy!             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Sun Sep 16, 2007 4:38 am I noticed this a while ago, but had no where to talk about it UNTIL NOW!!! :twisted: In Episode 23, we see tha Hikari owns a Sega Saturn. Here is some screencaps: This shot of the system clearly shows it as a Sega Saturn. It also shows that Hikari also owns a Sega TV somehow. Hikari's stack of games including "Poyo Poyo" and "Winning Fighter". Asuka uses the distinct Sega Saturn Controler, notable from a Genesis (Mega Drive) controler by the blue "start" button and the shoulder buttons. In any case, this is most likely product placement, as Sega sponsored Evangelion and the Saturn was new at the time of Eva's first air date in Japan (1995). However this causes some anachronisms for the Eva timeline, which I will ramble on about now. In the "real" world, the Sega Saturn debuted on November 22, 1994 in Japan. (thanks, wikipedia!) If the time line of video game consoles in eva world is congruent to that of realtime, then this gives the Sega Saturn plenty of time to be produced in mass numbers before everything goes all to shit in the year 2000. However, it reamins unlikely for Hikari to have one based on several factors, one being the age of the system. Saturns were discontinued in 1999, meaning the newest Saturn Hikari could own would be 16 years old. Older than Hikari herself! Unless her older sister (or her parents, I can't remember if she has any or not) is a collector of vintage games from the era before Second Impact, her owning such an old system seems unlikely. And even then, why would in be in Hikari's room and throw haphazardly on the floor? Wouldn't it be a rare collectable, or atleast something from the "old world" worth taking care of. Speaking of which,we are told that Second Impact flooded a great deal of the earth, killed almost half of Mankind and caused large scale Wars. Much of Japan is now underwater. If all this is true, how much of the stuff from "the world that was" would have survived the cataclysm and how much of it would be available and affordable to tjhe masses? My guess would be "not much", espically not an expensive, 16 year old video game system and a stack of vintage games. In closing, I'm just wanna say that I am a huge nerd and apologize for my tangent adventure into nerdom. XD

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Sun Sep 16, 2007 4:56 am It's not really much of a stretch to think Hikari could get her hands on an old video game system. I still play the NES I've had since 1988 (19 Years). Maybe they stopped making Video Games after 2I, and the only way to get your fix is to go old school?

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sun Sep 16, 2007 6:32 am That was an interesting read, shinesman_cobalt! I noticed that the Sega Saturn seemed a little out of place in Eva. But, I suppose that after 2I, games companies didn't .... bother anymore? This reminds of the PlayStation I saw in Cowboy Bebop! Cowboy Bebop is set, like, 50 years in the future and on different planets, so it seems weird that there'd be a PlayStation there!

[]:             Quote Post by ThroneofDravaris » Sun Sep 16, 2007 7:01 am It's entirely possible considering the state of the world that the Saturn, Platstation and N64 were the last consoles to be made in the Evangelion universe. With that in mind, it's not such a great stretch that someone would hold onto a 16+ year old system. Although why she would buy a Saturn over the obviously superior Playstation is beyond me. EDIT: Way for me to not read the other posts and add absolutely nothing to the thread.

[]:             Quote Post by Operation Yashima » Sun Sep 16, 2007 9:05 am CitizenGeek wrote: I noticed that the Sega Saturn seemed a little out of place in Eva. But, I suppose that after 2I, games companies didn't .... exist anymore?

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Sep 16, 2007 9:36 am I don't find it particularly odd that Hikari would have an antique console at home; I can attest from personal experience that older electronics tend to be more durable than the new stuff. In any case, I think a more important question is why the Eva pistol happens to look exactly like an XBOX HUEG DESERT EAGLE.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Sun Sep 16, 2007 10:32 am Because she has an old system and the show was made before the current stuff came out. WoooOOOOOOoooo! EDIT: Don't forget product placement.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Sep 16, 2007 10:41 am I've also heard that vintage Apple Powerbooks are quite widely used in 2015. It's no surprise that a product placement like the Sega Saturn found its way into Evangelion. After all, Sega was a sponsor, and there was at least three Evangelion games on the Saturn at the time.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sun Sep 16, 2007 10:45 am The Eva Monkey wrote: I've also heard that vintage Apple Powerbooks are quite widely used in 2015.

[]:             Quote Post by zeaven » Sun Sep 16, 2007 10:54 am shinesman_cobalt wrote: ...which I will ramble on about now.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Sep 16, 2007 12:14 pm So, who wants to bet on what the new console Hikari will have in Rebuild is going to be? =D Sega no longer makes its own consoles, and X-Box isn't made in Japan, so I'm going to hazard to guess that she's going to be playing a Wii.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sun Sep 16, 2007 12:38 pm I'm a bit of a 360 fanboy, so I'm gonna hate myself forever for saying this but...maybe a PS3?

[]:             Quote Post by King_Panther13 » Sun Sep 16, 2007 12:58 pm Indigo Arcangel wrote: I'm a bit of a 360 fanboy, so I'm gonna hate myself forever for saying this but...maybe a PS3?

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sun Sep 16, 2007 1:11 pm They'll probably use a Wii for the synchronization between Shinji and Asuka. That could work well!

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sun Sep 16, 2007 2:03 pm NAveryW wrote: So, who wants to bet on what the new console Hikari will have in Rebuild is going to be? =D Sega no longer makes its own consoles, and X-Box isn't made in Japan, so I'm going to hazard to guess that she's going to be playing a Wii.

With that in mind, it's not such a great stretch that someone would hold onto a 16+ year old system. Although why she would buy a Saturn over the obviously superior Playstation is beyond me. I noticed that the Sega Saturn seemed a little out of place in Eva. But, I suppose that after 2I, games companies didn't .... bother anymore? This reminds of the PlayStation I saw in Cowboy Bebop! Cowboy Bebop is set, like, 50 years in the future and on different planets, so it seems weird that there'd be a PlayStation there!

[]:             Quote Post by shinesman_cobalt » Sun Sep 16, 2007 2:21 pm NAveryW wrote: So, who wants to bet on what the new console Hikari will have in Rebuild is going to be? =D Sega no longer makes its own consoles, and X-Box isn't made in Japan, so I'm going to hazard to guess that she's going to be playing a Wii.

[]:             Quote Post by Indigo Arcangel » Sun Sep 16, 2007 2:30 pm When it comes down to it, we're all forgetting one thing..THE WORLD OF NGE ISN'T REAL!!! p.s I just realised how hypocritical that was of me. Sorry :cry:

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Sep 16, 2007 4:11 pm Heh, I've got an NES that's older than me. (3 or 4 years, actually) I don't know how long I've had it, but its what I used to play all the time before the N64 came out. And I wouldn't be surprised if there were people that sold vintage electronics. My friend's brother always has a few gadgets from the mid-80's that he's collected. Besides, you have 15 years after a world changing catastrophe, its possible to enter the Age of the Wii a few years late, I mean, we have Evas that work with mind power, surely they can make a game system with motion sensitive controls...
